task: Stage-by-stage debug the vince-skill pipeline (guidance -> engineer -> conductor+attacker) against a lightweight 3-fixture eval, proving each stage's behavior with a discriminating check; zipper is out of scope.
loop_altitude_rationale: Edits three load-bearing but reversible (git) pipeline skills in a strictly sequential chain (build harness -> guidance -> engineer -> conductor); single-agent. The attacker multi-agent fan-out lives INSIDE the conductor stage's own design, not as parallel debug stages, so no large-altitude debug fan-out is needed.

selection_log.why: every stage has a fast runnable check that answers done without a human reading output: selftest.mjs (scorer discriminates), run_all.mjs --stage X exit code, attacker validate_attack_records.mjs / check_battery_clean.mjs. Done is machine-decidable.

seam1: the eval harness + both-polarity fixtures (handed to every debug stage); seam2: the richer handoff-spec.json (guidance -> engineer); seam3: the built, testable skill + working pipeline (engineer -> conductor). Each upstream check runs before the downstream stage exists (harness selftest before any fix; spec scorer before engineer is touched); each artifact is consumed unchanged; no two stages edit the same surface in conflict.

each stage's dominant failure mode is a plausible-looking change that does not actually fix the behavior (a scorer that does not discriminate; a guidance edit that does not elicit; a build that is vacuously green; an attacker wired in the wrong order). plan_execute_verify's verify-each-step against the stage check is the pattern that catches that mode; the check is the cheapest one that still fails on it.

reversible (per-skill git commits, debug runs on repo working copies, design-only persist). Gates are strong: selftest discrimination, the engineer mutation/vacuity probe, the attacker validator. The one calibration-subjective element (guidance's question trigger) is gated by both-polarity fixtures + a held-out fixture + a blind-judge maker/checker + human sign-off at the stage-1 boundary, so it does not warrant in_the_loop per-iteration approval on the strong-gate stages.
the four stages are strictly sequential — each depends on the prior's artifact (harness -> specs -> builds -> pipeline) — so there is no pair of independent debug stages to fan out. Single agent.

selection_log.answer: per-stage caps + upstream loopback routing; outer budget 4 + escalates
selection_log.why: harness escalates (cannot build a discriminating check -> human); guidance loops back to the harness (a failing check often means the scorer/fixtures need recalibration); engineer loops back to guidance (untestable build => spec still thin, the user's #2-caused-by-#1 chain); conductor loops back to engineer. Risk guards cover overfitting, error amplification, pipeline-breakage, premature attacker, zipper scope creep, permission blast.

definition_of_done.goal: evals/skill-pipeline-debug/{run_all.mjs,selftest.mjs} + a both-polarity fixture set (>=2 under-specified gap-fixtures each with an answer-key, >=1 fully-specified complete-fixture, 1 held-out) exist, and selftest proves each stage-scorer DISCRIMINATES: it RED-flags a planted thin/under-spec artifact AND GREEN-flags a planted rich artifact, for the guidance, engineer, and conductor scorers

feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: any stage-scorer passes a deliberately thin/placeholder artifact (or fails a known-rich one) — i.e. it does not discriminate — so selftest exits non-zero
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: a one-sided selftest that only asserts the rich golden artifact PASSES but never asserts a thin one FAILS — it would green a no-op scorer. Ruled out: selftest requires BOTH a known-bad->FAIL and a known-good->PASS assertion per stage-scorer (guidance/engineer/conductor)

failure: a discriminating scorer cannot be built for a stage because the target behavior is not observable in any produced artifact

definition_of_done.goal: for every GAP fixture, guidance emits a clarifying-questions artifact whose questions cover >=K of that fixture's answer-key context gaps AND a handoff-spec that fills all 8 recommended_design units + intent in/out-of-scope concretely with <=1 residual vague blocking_unknown; for the COMPLETE fixture it emits NO spurious questions and a fully-resolved spec; the held-out fixture also passes — graded by run_all.mjs --stage guidance exit 0

feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: a gap-fixture run produces a thin spec that dumps the required context into vague blocking_unknowns and emits NO clarifying questions (the current failure mode), or the complete-fixture run emits spurious questions / leaves design units as placeholders — the scorer exits non-zero
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: guidance overfits the 2-3 training fixtures — hard-codes their answer-keys, or always emits a fixed question list to trivially 'cover' any gap. Ruled out by: (a) a held-out 4th fixture not used during iteration that must also pass; (b) the both-polarity assertion — always-asking fails the complete-fixture no-spurious-questions check; (c) the maker/checker blind judge reads a spec cold for genuine concreteness, not keyword presence

failure: the question trigger cannot satisfy both polarities (under-asks on a gap fixture OR over-asks on the complete fixture) after the cap
the spec scorer is found mis-calibrated, so the failure is in the harness not in guidance

definition_of_done.goal: for each fixture's richer spec, vince-skill-engineer produces a built skill whose own evals/run_all.mjs exits 0, with a real .skill-engineer/red/red.log (FAIL lines), a build-report with verification.harness_ran=true and command_output carrying PASS lines; AND a mutation/vacuity probe passes — planting a defect in the built skill flips its harness to RED, then restores — graded by run_all.mjs --stage engineer exit 0

feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: engineer produces a skill whose harness has no real assertions — the mutation probe does NOT flip the built harness to RED — or the build-report claims harness_ran=true while command_output has no PASS lines; the scorer exits non-zero
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: the built harness is green but only contains tautological 'grep SKILL.md' tests. Ruled out by the mutation probe (a tautology survives a mutation of the actual logic, so the probe catches it) plus reusing skill-conductor round6's validateScriptHarness check and the engineer skill's own no-tautological-tests rule

failure: the built skill stays untestable across the cap because the spec is still too thin (root cause is upstream, loop back to guidance)
engineer cannot produce a non-vacuous harness for a fixture

definition_of_done.goal: conductor's pipeline on a fixture (a) does NOT invoke attacker until guidance re-audit passes — a fixture engineered to FAIL re-audit shows zero attacker invocation; (b) once re-audit passes, invokes attacker whose records pass skills/attacker/scripts/validate_attack_records.mjs and whose ledger passes check_battery_clean.mjs; (c) folds the attacker verdict into the effective verdict via min() — a planted attacker-found defect lowers it; (d) skill-conductor/evals/round6_pipeline_checks.mjs stays green and zipper's own evals still pass — graded by run_all.mjs --stage conductor exit 0

feedback_signal.falsifiable_when: conductor invokes attacker BEFORE guidance re-audit passes (premature, on the fail-audit fixture), OR skips attacker after re-audit passes (gate unwired), OR a planted attacker-found defect does NOT lower the effective verdict (verdict ignored) — the scorer exits non-zero
feedback_signal.passing_but_wrong: conductor 'calls' attacker but it runs as a no-op emitting an empty or forged-clean ledger. Ruled out: validate_attack_records.mjs requires the independence_attestation and check_battery_clean.mjs --need N requires real exercised attacks; the fixture plants a defect the attacker MUST find, so an empty/forged ledger fails the 'attacker found the planted defect' assertion

failure: the ordering gate cannot be wired without changing attacker's own contract (out of scope)
making guidance trigger-interactive irreconcilably breaks conductor's autonomous end-to-end run

maker_checker.scope: a fresh-context reviewer sees ONLY the produced artifacts (specs, built skills, conductor-logs) + the per-stage checks — never the iteration history or the fixtures' answer-keys — and flags: (a) a guidance spec that looks concrete but is vague on a real gap or emitted no question where context was insufficient; (b) a built harness that is green but vacuous; (c) a conductor run that calls attacker out of order or ignores its verdict. Correctness / stated-requirement gaps only

harness_primitives: git per-skill commits + worktree isolation for reversible edits to skill-guidance / skill-engineer / skill-conductor

a both-polarity fixture set: >=2 under-specified gap-fixtures (each with an answer-key of must-elicit context) + >=1 fully-specified complete-fixture + 1 held-out fixture (overfitting guard) + 1 deliberately-fails-re-audit fixture (a built-but-deficient skill that reaches conductor Step 5 yet fails guidance re-audit — exercises the attacker ordering gate's no-call branch)

deterministic scorers that GRADE produced artifacts (the agent invokes the LLM skills each iteration; the scorer reads the resulting spec / build-report / conductor-log) — selftest proves the scorers discriminate before any debugging starts
a mutation/vacuity probe utility: plant a defect in a built skill, assert its harness flips RED, then restore
external scratch ledger .loop/skill-pipeline-debug/run-state.md tracking which stage is green across context resets

stop_conditions.success: all four stage checks pass in dependency order on the full fixture set INCLUDING the held-out fixture; guidance trigger-elicits on gaps without over-asking on complete input; engineer builds a non-vacuous testable skill from the richer spec; conductor gates attacker behind a passing re-audit and folds its verdict via min(); conductor round6 + zipper evals stay green; zipper source untouched
failure: any stage exhausts its max_iterations without passing its check
a downstream stage forces a redesign of an already-accepted upstream stage more than twice
the held-out fixture fails while the training fixtures pass (overfitting detected)
escalate: the guidance question trigger cannot satisfy both polarities after the stage cap
making guidance ask questions irreconcilably breaks conductor's autonomous pipeline run
the attacker ordering gate would require changing attacker's own contract
a stage's target behavior turns out not machine-observable in any artifact (no discriminating check buildable)

risk_guards.risk: reward hacking / test overfitting to the 2-3 fixtures (guidance/engineer learn the fixtures, or guidance always-asks to trivially cover gaps)
risk_guards.mitigation: held-out 4th fixture + both-polarity tests + blind-judge maker/checker; scorers reward resolved-or-targeted-question, not keyword presence

risk_guards.risk: error amplification across the guidance->engineer->conductor chain (a wrong guidance fix poisons engineer's eval)
risk_guards.mitigation: stage gates run in dependency order; an engineer failure loops back to guidance rather than head-banging in place

risk_guards.risk: breaking the autonomous pipeline (trigger-interactive guidance hangs conductor's hands-off run)
risk_guards.mitigation: stage-3 check asserts conductor runs end-to-end without blocking on input; guidance must elicit ONLY on a detected gap and defer/auto-assume in pipeline mode; escalate if irreconcilable

risk_guards.risk: premature / wasted attacker invocation (attacking a skill that cannot even pass guidance re-audit)
risk_guards.mitigation: stage-3 ordering gate: attacker invoked ONLY after guidance re-audit passes; falsifiable by the fail-audit fixture showing zero attacker calls

risk_guards.risk: context drift / scope creep into zipper (the user said zipper is fine)
risk_guards.mitigation: zipper is OUT of scope; only a light regression that zipper's own evals stay green; no edits to zipper source

risk_guards.risk: permission blast radius (edits 3 load-bearing installed skills)
risk_guards.mitigation: per-skill git commits, debug runs against the repo working copies under skills/, no redeploy to ~/.claude or ~/.agents until the human accepts at the outer gate
